acceptanceCriteria: cmp dist/rigor.cjs .rigor/rigor-ci.cjs succeeds on the merged tree (the committed CI bundle is byte-identical to the committed dist bundle).
node .rigor/rigor-ci.cjs ci --base <base> --head <head> on a real PR range produces non-empty JSON and enforces evidence linkage; recorded as manual verification in .rigor/evidence/GH-44/.

A deterministic, repository-specific mechanism makes byte-drift between dist/rigor.cjs and .rigor/rigor-ci.cjs fail loudly: a rigor release-check finding (ci-bundle-sync) and a node:test check that runs under npm run test:all, both gated on both files existing so consumer repositories without a source dist/ tree are not broken.

A negative fixture proves the drift check fails when only dist/rigor.cjs is mutated, and an entry-guard smoke proves node .rigor/rigor-ci.cjs ci produces non-empty JSON (the #29 entry-guard failure mode).

docs/release.md states the regeneration rule (any release whose dist/rigor.cjs bytes change must regenerate the CI bundle) and documents the new release-check finding and its consumer-repository false-positive behavior.
docs/threat-model.md wording on CI verifier freshness reflects the new deterministic local drift detection where needed.
npm run test:all, npm run bench, claude plugin validate . --strict, and git diff --check all pass.
constraints: Implement only Issue #44 Scope and Acceptance criteria; add no unrelated features.
Do not change the write-once behavior of setup/upgrade for consumer repositories (Non-goal).
Do not implement consumer-side provenance/authentication of the bundle (Non-goal; that is #26/ADR 0001).
The drift check must be repository-specific and must not fail in consumer repositories where dist/rigor.cjs does not exist alongside .rigor/rigor-ci.cjs.
Do not delete, weaken, or skip any pre-existing test.

Because src/release.ts changes, dist/rigor.cjs must be rebuilt with npm run build and committed (repo release flow), and .rigor/rigor-ci.cjs must then be a byte-identical copy of that rebuilt dist/rigor.cjs, not hand-edited.
Do not modify schema files, public CLI surface, or the ci/verification artifact formats.
stopConditions: an irreversible or external write is needed
credentials or personal data are encountered
